name: Oregon SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Oregon's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries. Administered by the Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS).
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 185% FPL for most households in Oregon

name: Oregon Health Plan (OHP / Medicaid)
description: Oregon's Medicaid program providing free health coverage for eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health, dental, and vision.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL under Medicaid expansion; children up to 305% FPL
languages: English, Spanish, Russian, Vietnamese, Chinese, Korean, and other languages

name: Oregon Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Oregon Employment Department.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Oregon Housing and Community Services (OHCS)
description: Provides affordable housing resources including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, homeless prevention, and manufactured housing services for Oregon residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households at or below 80% AMI

name: Legal Aid Services of Oregon
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Oregonians in matters including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and immigration.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% FPL or below; some programs may have different limits
languages: English, Spanish, Russian, and other languages

name: Oregon Law Center
description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income Oregonians focusing on systemic advocacy and legal representation in matters affecting poverty communities.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and communities across Oregon

name: WorkSource Oregon
description: Oregon's workforce development system offering job search assistance, training programs, resume help, and career counseling through centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: All Oregon residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Oregon LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Administered through community action agencies across Oregon.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and young children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of state median income

name: Oregon 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Oregonians with local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All Oregon residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and 150+ languages via interpreter

name: Oregon Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving all of Oregon and Clark County, Washington. Distributes food through a network of 1,400+ partner agencies and operates the statewide food bank network.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in Oregon

name: Oregon Department of Veterans' Affairs
description: Assists Oregon veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits including healthcare, disability compensation, education, home loans, and emergency aid.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in Oregon

name: Oregon Crisis Line
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress for Oregon residents.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Oregon experiencing a mental health crisis
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Oregon ERDC (Employment Related Day Care)
description: Oregon's childcare assistance program helping low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work. One of the most generous childcare programs in the US.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children who need childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 200% FPL

name: Oregon TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families)
description: Cash assistance program providing monthly benefits and supportive services to low-income families with children while helping parents achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children who meet work or activity requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: Oregon WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for eligible participants.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5

name: TriMet (Portland Metro Transit)
description: Public transit system serving the Portland metropolitan area with bus, MAX light rail, and WES commuter rail service. Offers reduced fare programs for low-income riders.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Portland metro area; reduced fares for low-income, seniors, and disabled
incomeLimitNotes: Honored Citizen fare for seniors 65+, disabled; reduced fare program for income below federal poverty level
hours: Varies by route; most routes 5:00 AM - 1:00 AM

name: Lane Transit District (Eugene-Springfield)
description: Public transit system serving the Eugene-Springfield metropolitan area with bus service and the EmX bus rapid transit line.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Eugene-Springfield area

name: Oregon Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, air sealing, and heating system upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Oregon Vocational Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services for Oregonians with disabilities including job training, placement assistance, assistive technology, and career counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Home Forward (Portland Housing Authority)
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Portland area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Portland metro area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Oregon Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for victims of domestic violence in Oregon.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in Oregon
languages: English, Spanish, and 200+ languages via interpreter

name: Oregon Paid Leave
description: Oregon's paid family and medical leave program allowing workers to take paid time off for family bonding, serious health conditions, and safe leave from domestic violence.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who have earned at least $1,000 in wages during the base year and need leave for qualifying reasons

name: Oregon Promise Grant
description: Provides tuition assistance for recent Oregon high school graduates and GED completers attending community college. Covers remaining tuition after other grants.
eligibilitySummary: Recent Oregon high school graduates or GED completers entering community college within 6 months
incomeLimitNotes: Reduced award for students with family AGI above $75,000

name: Oregon Student Aid (OSAC)
description: Administers state grants, scholarships, and financial aid programs for Oregon students including the Oregon Opportunity Grant for low-income students.
eligibilitySummary: Oregon residents pursuing post-secondary education at eligible institutions
incomeLimitNotes: Oregon Opportunity Grant targets students with expected family contribution of $0

name: Multnomah County Joint Office of Homeless Services (Portland)
description: Coordinates homeless services in the Portland area including emergency shelter, rapid rehousing, prevention, and supportive housing programs.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing or at risk of homelessness in Multnomah County

name: Central City Concern (Portland)
description: Comprehensive nonprofit in Portland providing housing, healthcare, addiction recovery, and employment services for individuals experiencing homelessness and poverty.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals experiencing homelessness, addiction, or poverty in the Portland area

name: Oregon Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income Oregon households. Includes additional Oregon-specific discount.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program

name: Oregon ONE (Oregon Eligibility) System
description: Oregon's online portal for applying to and managing public assistance programs including OHP, SNAP, TANF, and childcare assistance in one application.
eligibilitySummary: All Oregon residents can use this portal to apply for multiple assistance programs
languages: English, Spanish, Russian, Vietnamese, Chinese, Korean

name: Oregon Head Start Pre-Kindergarten
description: Federally funded early childhood education programs promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income Oregon families.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; foster and homeless children automatically eligible

name: Salem-Keizer Transit (Cherriots)
description: Public transit system serving the Salem-Keizer metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Salem-Keizer area

name: Oregon Rental Assistance - Emergency Housing Assistance
description: Provides emergency rental assistance to prevent eviction and homelessness for Oregon tenants experiencing financial hardship.
eligibilitySummary: Renters at risk of eviction or homelessness who have experienced financial hardship
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 80% AMI
